mutations.change: Use the nearest hit sector for every floor and ceiling pixel instead of selecting the sector interval at reconstructed depth.
mutations.reason: Step and window scenes deliberately place distinct far flats and lights behind the first open portal.

mutations.change: Suppress upper and lower portal wall pieces and fill their projected spans from the far sector instead.
mutations.reason: The window freezes nonempty independently textured upper and lower projected regions.

mutations.change: Treat SKY1 as an ordinary ceiling flat and retain the upper wall between adjacent sky sectors.
mutations.reason: The sky fixture requires upper-piece suppression and camera-mapped full-bright sky pixels.

mutations.change: Apply the current sector light COLORMAP band to sky pixels instead of the fixed full-bright sky policy.
mutations.reason: Near and far sky sectors have intentionally divergent light values but identical expected sky sampling.

mutations.change: Always sample the first named animation asset and ignore the persisted game tic for wall and flat animation.
mutations.reason: The animation fixture uses tic seventeen to select noninitial flat and wall frames in different-period groups.

mutations.change: Read a constant zero tic rather than the bound GAME_SESSIONS current tic when choosing animation frames.
mutations.reason: The reviewed nonzero tic and wrap probes distinguish persisted tic selection from a fixed frame.

mutations.change: Discard seg offset and both signed sidedef offsets when calculating upper and lower texture coordinates.
mutations.reason: The offset scene combines nonzero seg, negative x, and positive y offsets with patterned texels.

mutations.change: Use default top and bottom wall origins even when upper- or lower-unpegged linedef flags are set.
mutations.reason: Separate upper and lower fixtures isolate both flag bits and their distinct vertical anchors.

mutations.change: Use remainder semantics for negative texture coordinates instead of floor_mod with the actual asset dimension.
mutations.reason: The signed offset fixture produces negative coordinates whose Euclidean wrap differs from Oracle MOD.

mutations.change: Project vertical spans at integer row edges rather than pixel centers row plus one half.
mutations.reason: Projected height boundaries deliberately cross reviewed rows where half-center classification changes the layer.

mutations.change: Advance the calculated light band by one before looking up every non-sky texel in COLORMAP.
mutations.reason: Every non-sky scene freezes patterned texels under independently selected sector light bands.

mutations.change: Perturb the light-band divisor so boundary light values enter the adjacent COLORMAP band.
mutations.reason: Fixture light levels bracket band boundaries and make exact floor and clamp semantics observable.

mutations.change: Duplicate one coordinate row while retaining the expected total row count by dropping a different coordinate.
mutations.reason: Bidirectional coordinate coverage and grouped uniqueness reject balanced drop-and-duplicate corruption.

mutations.change: Change one canonical palette index after composition while leaving all layer and cardinality metadata unchanged.
mutations.reason: Complete 64,000-byte SHA-256 values detect corruption outside the listed visible spot probes.

mutations.change: Loop over screen pixels or portal pieces in PL/SQL instead of deriving the frame with relational SQL.
mutations.reason: The charter forbids procedural pixel and wall loops and assigns rendering decisions to set-based SQL.

mutations.change: Use a recursive common table expression to carry clip bounds between portal hits.
mutations.reason: The render path explicitly forbids recursive WITH and requires analytic running clip bounds.

mutations.change: Construct renderer SQL dynamically with inlined pose, tic, or texture values rather than session binds and table rows.
mutations.reason: Dynamic SQL and inline runtime values are prohibited in rendering and weaken stable cursor behavior.

mutations.change: Embed visible scene names, expected hashes, fixture poses, or evaluator paths to special-case reviewed observations.
mutations.reason: Source auditing rejects evaluator coupling and transformed held-back scenes require general relational behavior.
